title: quercus neumannii
intro: Quercus neumannii, also known as Neumann's oak, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fagaceae, native to Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Quercus neumannii have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
